mission.title: kube-rs: `kube-derive` does not create required `schemas` field for CustomResourceDefinition
mission.description: `kube-derive` does not create required `schemas` field for CustomResourceDefinition . Requested by 5+ users.

steps.title: Check current kube-rs project
steps.description:
Verify your kube-rs version and configuration:
```bash
cargo --version
rustc --version
grep kube Cargo.toml 2>/dev/null
```
This feature requires a Rust development environment with the kube-rs crate as a dependency.

steps.title: Review kube-rs configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant kube-rs configuration:
```bash
grep -A5 '\[dependencies\]' Cargo.toml
cargo tree | grep kube
```
With `apiextensions.k8s.io/v1` the definition of a structural schema is mandatory for CustomResourceDefinitions, while in `v1beta1` this is still optional.

steps.title: Apply the fix for `kube-derive` does not create required `schemas` field for…
steps.description:
Schema is generated with `schemars`.

Also fixed the location of `subresources` and `additionalPrinterColumns`.

---

For deriving CRD v1, the spec struct must include `schemars::JsonSchema`. ~The schemas for subresources are currently not generated, but it should be possible to add the status
```yaml
apply error: ErrorResponse { status: "Failure", message: "Incorrect version specified in apply patch. Specified patch version: apiextensions.k8s.io/v1beta1, expected: apiextensions.k8s.io/v1", reason: "BadRequest", code: 400 }
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
cargo build 2>&1 | tail -20
cargo test 2>&1 | tail -20
```
Confirm the feature described in "`kube-derive` does not create required `schemas` field for…" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
Schema is generated with `schemars`.

Also fixed the location of `subresources` and `additionalPrinterColumns`.

---

For deriving CRD v1, the spec struct must include `schemars::JsonSchema`.
codeSnippets: apply error: ErrorResponse { status: "Failure", message: "Incorrect version specified in apply patch. Specified patch version: apiextensions.k8s.io/v1beta1, expected: apiextensions.k8s.io/v1", reason: "BadRequest", code: 400 }
Fortunately, the workaround is easy, just create the YAML myself:

prerequisites.description: A Rust development environment with cargo and the kube-rs crate as a project dependency.
